free_text: In each case the stack of foils was mounted
inside an electrically-suppressed Faraday cup to
give a reliable measurement of beam fluence.
Stacks of 25 mu-m thick Co foils were irradiated.
In the first, an 85 MeV beam was used with a stack
of 18 foils, covering the energy range from 85 MeV
in the second, a 50.1 MeV beam and 8 foils were used,
covering the range from 50 MeV down to 30 MeV;
in the third, a 38.2 MeV beam and 8 foils covered
the energy range from 38 MeV down to below the
do not include the overall uncertainty in the absolute
charge-collection efficiency of the Faraday cup.
